title: Influenza Antiviral Medications: Summary for Clinicians | Influenza (Flu) | CDC
text:

For hospitalized patients who cannot tolerate or absorb oral or enterically administered oseltamivir because of suspected or known gastric stasis, malabsorption, or gastrointestinal bleeding, intravenous peramivir may be considered (Lee, 2017; de Jong, 2014; Ison, 2014; Ison, 2013). There are insufficient data to support general use of inhaled zanamivir and intravenous peramivir in patients with severe influenza disease. There are no available data from clinical trials on use of baloxavir treatment in patients with severe influenza disease who are not hospitalized. Oral oseltamivir and oral baloxavir are available treatment options for patients at higher risk for influenza complication depending upon their underlying conditions and age (Table 1). Data on use of peramivir or zanamivir are very limited in high-risk outpatients with influenza. 
Influenza (Flu) 
Influenza (flu) is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses that infect the nose, throat, and lungs.
